# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application built with TypeScript, using Supabase as the backend-as-a-service platform. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Tech Stack Details
- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and dark mode
- **UI Components**: shadcn/ui (Radix UI primitives)
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for auth
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL database, authentication, edge functions)
- **Rich Text**: TipTap editor for policy documents
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Role-based access control (CEO, HR Manager, Team Lead, Employee)
- Route protection with `ProtectedRoute` component
- Permission checking via `canAccess` utility function

### Data Flow
- TanStack Query for API calls and caching
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Real-time subscriptions for live data
- Edge functions for complex business logic (payroll, notifications)

### Component Structure
- Atomic design principles with reusable UI components
- Feature-based organization (components grouped by domain)
- Custom hooks for business logic separation
- TypeScript interfaces for type safety

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Entities
- **employees**: User profiles with role assignments
- **companies**: Multi-tenant company data
- **evaluations**: Quarterly performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations**: Daily feedback system
- **leave_requests**: Leave management with balances
- **payroll**: Salary processing and payslips
- **projects**: Project management and client relationships
- **invoices**: Billing and financial tracking
- **hr_policies**: Document management

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies with role-based permissions
- Evaluations link employees to managers with scoring systems
- Leave requests track balances and approval workflows
- Projects connect clients, employees, and financial data

## Component Architecture

### Layout System
- `AppLayout`: Main application wrapper with sidebar navigation
- `AppSidebar`: Collapsible navigation with role-based menu items
- `TopBar`: User actions and notifications
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

### UI Component Library
- 40+ reusable components from shadcn/ui
- Consistent theming with CSS custom properties
- Dark/light mode support
- Accessible components built on Radix UI

### Feature Components
- **Employee Management**: Profile views, forms, searchable selects
- **Evaluation System**: Timeline views, rating components, feedback forms
- **Leave Management**: Calendar integration, balance tracking, approval flows
- **Finance**: Invoice generation, payroll processing, reporting

## Development Workflow

### Code Quality
- TypeScript for type safety
- ESLint for code linting
- Prettier for code formatting
- Vitest for unit testing
- Playwright for E2E testing

### Build & Deployment
- Vite for optimized production builds
- Environment-based configuration
- Supabase CLI for database migrations
- Edge functions for serverless backend logic

## Key Files & Directories

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx`: Main routing and providers
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page component

### Core Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

### Business Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation utilities
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Database client and types

### Components
- `src/components/ui/`: Reusable UI primitives
- `src/components/layout/`: Application layout components
- `src/components/[feature]/`: Domain-specific components

## Supabase Integration

### Client Setup
- Supabase client initialized in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`
- Type-safe database operations with generated types
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates

### Edge Functions
- `supabase/functions/`: Serverless functions for complex operations
- Payroll generation, invoice PDF creation, email notifications
- Written in TypeScript with Deno runtime

### Database
- PostgreSQL with Row Level Security (RLS)
- Automated migrations in `supabase/migrations/`
- Real-time enabled tables for live features

## Performance Considerations

### Optimization Strategies
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- Query caching with TanStack Query
- Bundle analysis and tree shaking
- Service worker for caching (future enhancement)

### Scalability
- Component memoization with React.memo
- Virtual scrolling for large lists
- Pagination for data-heavy views
- CDN-ready asset optimization

## Security Measures

### Frontend Security
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- XSS protection with React's built-in sanitization
- CSRF protection via Supabase
- Secure environment variable handling

### Data Protection
- Row Level Security in Supabase
- Encrypted sensitive data storage
- Audit logging for critical operations
- Role-based data access controls

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing
- Vitest for component and utility testing
- React Testing Library for component testing
- Mock Service Worker for API mocking

### Integration Testing
- Playwright for end-to-end testing
- Supabase test database setup
- CI/CD pipeline integration

## Deployment & DevOps

### Environment Management
- Environment-specific configurations
- Supabase project per environment
- Automated migration deployment
- Rollback strategies for database changes

### Monitoring & Analytics
- Error tracking with Sentry (recommended)
- Performance monitoring
- User analytics integration
- Database performance monitoring

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the Beudox HR codebase and making informed decisions about development, maintenance, and feature additions.
